questions.question: What is the remainder when $17$ is divided by $5$?
The ancient Egyptians built a pyramid with $180$ stone blocks in each row. How many rows can be made if each row must have a multiple of $10$ blocks?
The Babylonians used a sexagesimal (base-$60$) number system. Is the number $120$ divisible by $4$ in this system?
What is the result of $43 \div 5$ with remainder?
The ancient Greeks used $12$ gods to represent the $12$ months of the year. If $36$ stones are to be divided equally among these $12$ gods, how many stones will each god receive?
A Mayan calendar has $365$ days in a year. If the days are divided into weeks of $7$ days, how many weeks are there in a year with remainder?
The ancient Chinese used the remainder theorem to solve problems. If $17$ is divided by $5$, what is the remainder?

The ancient Sumerians used a system of mathematics that included long division. Perform long division to find the quotient and remainder of $63 \div 9$. Show your work and write your answer as $\boxed{[quotient \text{ with remainder } remainder]}$.

In ancient Egypt, a farmer has $48$ baskets of wheat to distribute among $8$ villages. If each village must receive an equal number of baskets, how many baskets will each village receive? Perform the division and write your answer as $\boxed{[answer]}$.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of understanding the concepts of divisibility and division algorithm.
Tip 2: Ensure students can apply the rules of divisibility for 2, 3, 5, 9, and 10.
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-world examples and historical contexts to practice problem-solving skills.
